name: Time-Dependent Pathfinding System
introduction: RMIT Algorithms and Analysis Course (Semester 1, 2026) | An interactive, high-performance pathfinding and routing engine implemented entirely from scratch in Python with zero external dependencies. The engine features custom-built data structures (binary min-heaps), dynamic obstacle avoidance, rush-hour traffic simulation (time-aware routing), and precomputed hub cache tables for optimal query speeds.
activities: Developed a custom binary MinHeap from scratch to bypass limitations in standard libraries, enabling efficient node distance updates and optimizing memory usage during graph traversals on a 19,600-node network.

Improved routing execution times by implementing a bidirectional search variant, successfully reducing the search space and node exploration compared to standard unidirectional Dijkstra across sparse and dense topologies.
Integrated a path-caching mechanism to store and instantly retrieve frequently requested routes, avoiding redundant calculations and minimizing CPU overhead for repetitive queries.
problemSolved: Routing engines often rely heavily on large third-party libraries and face scaling challenges when handling dynamic obstacles or hourly traffic shifts. Smart Path Finder solves this by building custom, optimized data structures (a binary min-heap), implementing bidirectional search to shrink the search space, and introducing a hub-based precomputation cache to speed up queries, all in a pure standard library Python implementation.
